_comment: Narration structures for 60-second gospel shorts. Each structure is an ordered set of timed beats. The engine generates one text block per beat; narration.md is the beats joined in order (blank line between). 'pace' tells the audio path which beat is a normal-pace spoken quote vs hurried narration. Add structures here and select with DEFAULT_STRUCTURE / the env override.

gospel-five-beat.name: Gospel Five-Beat
gospel-five-beat.description: Retention-optimised, gospel-safe spine: Hook -> Point -> Proof -> Conviction -> Landing. Stakes are anchored in truth about God and Christ (not self-interest), and the close invites a response to Jesus rather than manufacturing pressure.

beats.name: Hook
beats.intent: Shock + immediate relevance. One line that names the viewer's real world and opens a loop they need closed. A genuine pattern interrupt, impossible to scroll past, and honest about what the short delivers.

beats.name: Point
beats.intent: One unbreakable biblical claim about God / who Jesus is. Stated plainly, no hedging. This is the single idea the whole short defends.

beats.name: Proof
beats.pace: KJV quote at NORMAL pace + narration
beats.intent: The evidence: the KJV verse or the scene that proves the Point. Quote the spoken words verbatim (this is the normal-pace anchor). Then honestly meet the single strongest objection a thoughtful skeptic would raise here - steel-man it, do not strawman it.

beats.name: Conviction
beats.intent: The stakes BEFORE GOD - why this pierces. Create holy tension (Hebrews 4:12 register), not information. Grace-anchored: NO gain/loss or self-interest framing ('what you get'), NO fear-selling, NO manufactured pressure. The Spirit convicts; the line exposes the heart honestly.

beats.name: Landing
beats.intent: Restate the Point in one line, then a direct invitation that lands on JESUS - come to Him, trust Him, follow Him - and a question that calls for a response to Christ. Grace, not coercion; warm, not cheesy.
